context.projectOverview: agent-bober: multi-agent autonomous build harness (TS ESM CLI + MCP). This spec adds a NEW src/telegram/ thin presentation adapter exposing the existing hub/inbox/approve-gate/calendar/medical features over a locally-run long-polling Telegram bot. No new domain logic.
relevantFiles: package.json (no Telegram lib present; execa present)

contract.title: Long-polling bot transport + user-id whitelist + outbound safe-summary funnel
contract.description: Create the src/telegram/ module and an `agent-bober telegram` CLI command that starts a locally-run long-polling getUpdates bot. Add a pure whitelist authorizer keyed on TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS that denies non-listed senders (replying with a denial that echoes their numeric id) and admits listed senders, and a single sendSafe outbound funnel through which every reply is sent — establishing the control-plane boundary. This sprint adds no task, hub, calendar, or medical domain logic.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) compiles the new src/telegram/ module to dist/ with zero TypeScript errors.
`npm run typecheck` reports zero type errors across the added src/telegram/*.ts and src/cli/commands/telegram.ts files.
A unit test for the whitelist authorizer returns deny for a sender id absent from TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS and allow for a sender id present in the comma-separated list.
A unit test asserts the denial reply produced for a non-listed sender contains that sender's exact numeric id as a substring.

A unit test confirms outbound replies are produced only via the sendSafe funnel: handlers return content and the Telegram send transport is invoked solely inside the funnel (verified with an injected transport spy).

Running `agent-bober telegram` with a valid TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN starts getUpdates long-polling; a non-whitelisted account receives the denial reply with its id, and a whitelisted account receives the /start help reply.

nonGoals: Do not implement any task, hub, calendar, or medical domain logic in this sprint — only transport, whitelist, and the outbound funnel.
Do not use webhooks, open an inbound listening port, or require a public HTTPS URL — long-polling getUpdates only.
Do not hardcode bot tokens or allowed user ids in source — read TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS from process.env.
Do not add more than one new npm dependency (the chosen Telegram Bot API library).
Do not let any handler call the Telegram send transport directly — all outbound text must go through the sendSafe funnel.
stopConditions: `npm run build` and `npm run typecheck` both exit zero, and the whitelist, denial-echo, and funnel unit tests pass under `npm test`.
The `agent-bober telegram` command is registered in src/cli/index.ts and prints usage via `agent-bober telegram --help`.
Starting the command with an empty TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN exits non-zero with a message naming the missing variable.
contract.definitionOfDone: A whitelisted operator runs `agent-bober telegram`, which opens a local long-polling getUpdates loop using the bot token from the environment — no server, no public URL, no inbound port. Messages from Telegram accounts whose numeric id is in TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS are admitted; every other account receives one denial reply that echoes its own numeric id and is otherwise ignored. All outbound text leaves through a single sendSafe funnel that later sprints extend, never by a handler calling the Telegram transport directly.
assumptions: Credentials are read from process.env (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS comma-separated), mirroring src/medical/whoop/whoop-token.ts:47-48.
Library choice is the generator's, constrained to a maintained lib supporting getUpdates long-polling; this is the only sprint that adds a dependency.
The Telegram transport is wrapped behind a small interface so handlers and the funnel are unit-testable without network access (provider-agnostic principle).
outOfScope: Task capture, prioritization, approvals, document upload, streaming, and digest delivery (Sprints 2-6).
Any inbound HTTP server or webhook receiver.
contract.generatorNotes: New module src/telegram/. Suggested files: bot.ts (long-poll loop + a thin transport wrapper interface, e.g. TelegramTransport with sendMessage/editMessage/getFile/answerCallback), whitelist.ts (pure parseAllowedUsers(env) + isAllowed(id, allowed) + denialReply(id)), outbound.ts (sendSafe(transport, chatId, content) — the single chokepoint). CLI: src/cli/commands/telegram.ts exporting registerTelegramCommand(program), imported and called in src/cli/index.ts exactly like registerMedicalCommand (see src/cli/index.ts:1-41 and src/cli/commands/medical.ts). Read env like src/medical/whoop/whoop-token.ts:47-48 (process.env["TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"], process.env["TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS"]). Library: pick ONE maintained Node Telegram Bot API library that supports getUpdates long-polling (grammY, telegraf, or node-telegram-bot-api) and state which in a code comment + commit message; this is the only sprint permitted to add a dependency. Keep the chosen SDK behind the transport wrapper so the rest of src/telegram/ depends on the wrapper, not the SDK (mirrors the providers/ adapter discipline). ESM: relative imports end in .js; use `import type` for type-only imports; no `any`; node:fs/promises only.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Confirm getUpdates long-polling is used and NO server/listen/createServer/webhook call exists (grep src/telegram/). Confirm credentials come from process.env, not literals. Confirm exactly one new dependency was added to package.json. Confirm the single-funnel invariant: grep that no handler/module calls the SDK send method outside outbound.ts/the transport wrapper. Run `npm run build`, `npm run typecheck`, and the whitelist/denial/funnel unit tests.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
